qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Shell command injection in execute_python_file via unsanitized filename with shell metacharacters
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding targets shell=True filename interpolation in autogpt/commands/execute_code.py::execute_python_file, while the advisory targets block execution endpoints that omit disabled-flag enforcement and allow the disabled BlockInstallationBlock to write and import attacker Python.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is the missing disabled-flag check in AutoGPT Platform block execution routes, enabling authenticated execution of the hardcoded disabled BlockInstallationBlock. The only qualifying final finding concerns a separate shell command injection in execute_python_file through unsanitized filenames.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-r277-3xc5-c79v describes /blocks/{block_id}/execute and external block execution endpoints calling get_block(block_id) and obj.execute(...) without checking obj.disabled.

The advisory's dangerous block is backend/blocks/block.py::BlockInstallationBlock with hardcoded id 45e78db5-03e9-447f-9395-308d712f5f08, disabled=True, and code written then executed via __import__().
command_injection_execute_python_file/vulnerability_report.json identifies autogpt/commands/execute_code.py lines 32-34 and 63-65, where a filename reaches shell=True command construction.
Session output scanned one qualifying final folder, command_injection_execute_python_file; macos_tts_command_injection is non_qualifying_vuln.
